DOM contract: required element ids/selec.btn-hint: the Hint button element (hint-and-review attaches a click handler here)
DOM contract: required element ids/selec.hint-display: the container element where the hint action label is written; cleared/refilled on each hint
DOM contract: required element ids/selec.review-panel: the container element where post-hand review rows are rendered; populated on phase transition into 'resolved'/'review'
DOM contract: required element ids/selec.review-panel.row-template: optional: a template element with structure { chosenAction, correctAction, matchIndicator } slots; if absent, hint-and-review constructs rows from scratch

renderHint(actionLabel).notes: Optional helper exposed by ui-render; if exposed, hint-and-review may call it instead of writing into hint-display directly. Either pathway is acceptable.

params.rows: Array<{ chosenAction: string, correctAction: string, matched: bool, handSnapshotSummary: string }>

renderReviewRows(rows).notes: Optional helper exposed by ui-render; analogous to renderHint.

notes: Data, not logic: ui-render owns the DOM containers; hint-and-review owns the action-label and review-row content. The contract gives hint-and-review either direct DOM ids OR helper functions to use — implementer may pick. No per-cell chart highlighting (IP4 lock); no chart embed (IP5 lock); rendered hint is a single label; review rows show chosen vs correct vs match indicator only.
